feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Africa's camels are dying from extreme heat as temperatures soar
entry.summary: Herders and experts tell the BBC how Africa's camels are suffering because of extreme heat.

entry.title: UPDATE: Health Officials Confirm Multiple Measles Cases in St. Mary's County; Possible Exposure at Three Locations Announced
entry.summary: UPDATE 7/22/2026: Maryland Department of Health confirms two measles cases in residents following recent out-of-state travel – Health officials are working to notify people who may have come into contact with the individuals The Maryland Department of Health …

entry.title: CRISPR–Cas regulates expression of embedded anti-phage defence systems
entry.summary: CRISPR–Cas systems transcriptionally tune diverse innate defences using CRISPR RNA-like guides to balance antiviral protection with fitness, and hyperactivate these defences when compromised, establishing a layered bacterial ‘immunity guard’ network.

entry.title: Experts reveal how to avoid deadly Legionnaires' disease as NYC outbreak kills 5
entry.summary: The New York City Legionnaires' disease outbreak has killed five and sickened 82, but doctors say key prevention steps can reduce exposure risk.

entry.title: Measles cases reach 35-year high
entry.summary: Presented by PhRMA {beacon} Healthcare Health Care PRESENTED BY The Big Story Measles cases at a 35-year high With the number of measles cases this year now surpassing last year’s annual total, the U.S. is seeing the highest number of cases in more than …

entry.title: Delaware's 4 new cases of measles meet criteria of "outbreak"
entry.summary: The number of recent confirmed cases of measles rose to four Wednesday, according to Delaware Public Health

entry.title: Ladera Ranch cluster of child cancer cases prompts residents to speak out at town hall
entry.summary: Residents of Orange County's Ladera Ranch spoke out at a town hall meeting held to discuss the investigation and response to an unusual cluster of child cancers in the community.

entry.title: Congo’s Ebola outbreak has killed over 1,000 people
entry.summary: More than 1,000 people have died from the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo, according to Africa’s top health body, a grim toll in what’s already been declared the fastest Ebola outbreak in history. The latest update from Congo’s Ministry of Health shows that a…

entry.title: Man Reveals The Sad Struggles Of Hypospadias, Disease Affecting 15 Million Men
entry.summary: "As far as we can tell, I'm one of the only people ever to share my story with lifelong hypospadias complications on social media," said Joel M.

entry.title: Vacation hot spot faces new CDC travel warning as outbreak cases continue climbing
entry.summary: The CDC issued a Level 2 Travel Health Notice for Costa Rica's Guanacaste Province after chikungunya cases surfaced near popular Playa Langosta. Travelers were told to take precautions.

entry.title: Yes, It’s Possible to Get Too Much Protein. These Signs Mean It’s Time to Cut Back.
entry.summary: Getting too much protein can crowd out fiber and carbs. Dietitians share warning signs to watch for and tips for balancing your intake.

entry.title: A strange transmissible cancer is spreading through the catfish in this lake
entry.summary: For more than a decade, there's been something fishy going on in a lake that straddles Vermont and the province of Quebec. It involves cancer, a kind of catfish, and — possibly — clues about how tumors metastasize.

entry.title: There Are People Trying to Get the “Explosive Diarrhea” on Purpose. I Know Exactly What They’re Thinking.
entry.summary: The “diarrhea diet” content is all over my algorithm.

entry.title: Engineered enzyme erases a stubborn mark of aging by up to 70% in human tissue samples
entry.summary: A biotech company called Revel Pharmaceuticals is looking into ways to reverse aging, and the company's science team, along with researchers from the company Calico and the University of Colorado, may be a step closer to realizing the so-called fountain of yo…

entry.title: 11 cyclosporiasis cases confirmed in Shelby County, health officials say
entry.summary: MEMPHIS, Tenn. — There are currently 11 confirmed cases of the cyclosporiasis parasite under investigation in Shelby County, health officials announced Wednesday. Four people were determined to have contracted the illness during international travel, six cont…

entry.title: The Best Lunch to Help Lose Visceral Fat, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: This belly fat-blasting lunch is packed with fiber, plant protein, healthy fat and antioxidants. It’s ready in 20 minutes so it’s a breeze to prepare.

entry.title: The 12-Minute Morning Routine That Builds More Strength Than an Hour at the Gym After 50
entry.summary: A certified trainer shares a 12-minute morning routine to build strength after 50, no gym required.

entry.title: Your husband (or wife) may increase your risk of dementia by up to 70%
entry.summary: How exactly can a close bond, partnership and mutual support hurt the health of your brain? Researchers have a few theories.

entry.title: I Have a Genetic Risk for Alzheimer’s. I Made These 5 Lifestyle Changes to Preserve My Brain Health
entry.summary: After her father’s Alzheimer’s diagnosis, one woman learned she had a rare genetic risk for the disease. She made major lifestyle changes to maintain brain health.

entry.title: Study finds special tomato drink may reduce inflammation in adults with obesity
entry.summary: A specially formulated tomato-soy beverage with concentrated lycopene and isoflavones reduced systemic inflammation markers in adults with obesity.

entry.title: No, You Shouldn’t Avoid Fruits and Vegetables Due to Cyclospora
entry.summary: A parasite called cyclospora has sickened thousands with explosive diarrhea. Here's what you must know and how to reduce your risk of infection.

entry.title: Nearly 1,000 people have died from Ebola in DR Congo
entry.summary: According to WHO figures, 999 people have died in DRC and two in Uganda due to the Bundibugyo strain of the virus.

entry.title: 6 Worst Foods for High Cholesterol, According to Cardiologists and Dietitians
entry.summary: Learn why dietitians and cardiologists say that solid cooking fats, processed meats, soda, fried chicken, ice cream and packaged pastries are the six worst foods for high cholesterol.

entry.title: Pancreatic cancer vaccine shows promise in first human trial, preventing disease in high-risk patients
entry.summary: A Johns Hopkins pancreatic cancer vaccine triggered immune responses in 90% of high-risk participants, with some cysts shrinking after vaccination.

entry.title: Fifth person dies in Legionnaires' disease outbreak in NYC neighborhood
entry.summary: The New York City health department said it has identified at least 82 cases of the disease in an outbreak in the Upper East Side of Manhattan.

entry.title: Newly identified molecule could help regulate sleep
entry.summary: Sleep is known to be essential for survival, as it allows both humans and other animals to rest, while the brain consolidates memories and clears out metabolic waste products. For many people worldwide, however, falling asleep at a reasonable time every eveni…

entry.title: Many kids don’t eat enough veggies. Amid a parasite outbreak in the US, what’s a parent to do?
entry.summary: An outbreak of cyclospora has some people eating less produce, but a global study found kids eat too few fruits and vegetables. Here’s what parents should know.

entry.title: Polio should have been eradicated by now. What’s plan B?
entry.summary: Amid funding cuts and political barriers, researchers are asking whether the campaign can ever succeed, and what to do next.

entry.title: Lung cancer in nonsmokers: "A women's health crisis"
entry.summary: Lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer death in American women — and a growing share of new cases are in women who've never smoked.

entry.title: Can you safely drink five cups of coffee a day? The American Heart Association weighs in
entry.summary: A new American Heart Association statement says many adults can safely drink more coffee than they might expect — but there are important caveats.

entry.title: The Best Breakfasts for Heart Health, According to a Cardiologist
entry.summary: Find out what sweet and savory options a cardiologist recommends for a heart-healthy breakfast, plus tips for choosing other heart-healthy options.

entry.title: Ultimate Limit of The Human Lifespan May Be Identified, Study Suggests
entry.summary: When the iconic rock band Queen asked, "Who wants to live forever?" the question was rhetorical, but for many people, the answer was "Yes".

entry.title: An HIV vaccine is within reach thanks to mRNA
entry.summary: ﻿But politics could derail the one thing to unlock it.

entry.title: Column | Here’s what an expert on accidental falls does every day to prevent them
entry.summary: Your risk of falling rises years before you’d expect.

entry.title: States want to bring Medicaid behind bars. Federal changes make that harder
entry.summary: Funding cuts and program changes required by President Trump's big budget law are forcing states to rethink bipartisan efforts to bring Medicaid into jails and prisons.

entry.title: The American Heart Association has a new number for daily coffee—and a warning if you go over it
entry.summary: New research suggests drinking one cup of coffee a day is actually worse for your health than drinking four—but that fifth shot could cause heart failure.

entry.title: Orange County residents demand answers over cluster of child cancer cases
entry.summary: Hundreds of Orange County residents gathered at a community meeting demanding answers after several cases of children in Ladera Ranch were diagnosed with a rare form of cancer in recent years. Since 2007, around nine children living in the upscale Ladera Ranc…

entry.title: New cancer strategy could stop tumors before resistance takes hold
entry.summary: Researchers are applying evolutionary theory to cancer by changing treatments before tumors have time to develop resistance. Mathematical models suggest that rapid, carefully timed switches between multiple therapies could improve cure rates.

entry.title: Magic mushrooms may reshape the brain long after the trip ends
entry.summary: A single dose of psilocybin produced measurable changes in brain activity and possible changes in brain structure that lasted for up to a month. Participants who experienced the greatest increase in flexible, varied brain activity also reported more personal …

entry.title: New Zealand just had its first bird flu case. Now its taking a rare step to protect native birds
entry.summary: The pacific nation is rushing to vaccinate 300 breeding pairs of its most endangered native birds to protect them from H5N1 strain of birdflu.

entry.title: 6 Supplements To Avoid Taking With Coffee
entry.summary: Drinking coffee may interfere with your body’s ability to absorb or use iron, calcium, vitamin D, B vitamins, zinc, and magnesium. Timing may matter.

entry.title: U.S. measles cases surpass last year's total: Tracker
entry.summary: They're at a level that hasn't been seen in 35 years.

entry.title: West Nile virus detected in South Carolina mosquitoes
entry.summary: West Nile virus has been detected in South Carolina, with infected mosquitoes found in several traps near the coast.

entry.title: Chase Field, Chandler mall among new measles exposure sites in Maricopa County
entry.summary: Attendees of a Chase Field concert and shoppers at an East Valley mall may have been exposed to measles, according to health officials.

entry.title: Venezuela confirms 3 people have died of hantavirus infections, with 2 more deaths suspected
entry.summary: Venezuela says three people died of infections of hantavirus but stressed there has been no evidence of human-to-human transmission in the South American country, which has rarely reported cases of the rodent-borne disease in humans. The Health Ministry said …

entry.title: U.S. measles cases hit 35-year record with months left in 2026
entry.summary: Data from Johns Hopkins University’s U.S. measles tracker shows that the nation reported 2,295 cases as of Tuesday. That count surpasses last year’s total of 2,289 cases.

entry.title: The 5 Best Fruits for Better Brain Health, According to Experts
entry.summary: Experts recommend five fruits that may help support brain health. Learn why blueberries, citrus fruits, dark grapes, strawberries and pomegranates are the top fruits to support cognitive function.

entry.title: Rabid bat found near zoo restaurant sparks urgent public health warning: 'Extremely serious'
entry.summary: A dead wild bat found near a San Diego Zoo Safari Park restaurant tested positive for rabies, marking the 19th such case in the county in 2026, said experts.

entry.title: Northern Nevada Public Health reports first cyclosporiasis case in Washoe County
entry.summary: Northern Nevada Public Health (NNPH) is reporting the first case of cyclosporiasis in Washoe County for 2026.

entry.title: Is Providence Your Primary Care Provider? Your Insurance May No Longer Cover Any Trips to Redwood Urgent Care
entry.summary: An abrupt policy change made last week by health care provider Providence briefly sent a local urgent care spiraling, though a Providence executive says that the change is mostly administrative.
